claim_text: DOE says data-center cooling can account for up to 40% of data-center energy usage overall.
notes: Direct support for cooling as a major operating burden.

claim_text: DOE says data-center electricity demand is projected to double or triple by 2028.
notes: Strong demand-side support for rising thermal loads.

claim_text: NVIDIA says hyperscale facilities can support over 135 kW per rack today, up from 20 kW historically.
notes: Supports the power-density shift making air cooling less viable.

claim_text: NVIDIA says liquid cooling is a key solution for next-generation AI infrastructure and that traditional air cooling struggles at these power levels.
notes: Supports liquid cooling as a necessity claim.

claim_text: NVIDIA contributed Blackwell liquid-cooling and thermal environment specifications to OCP to support higher compute density and networking bandwidth.
notes: Supports that cooling is now a design-spec layer of the ecosystem.

claim_text: DOE COOLERCHIPS funding explicitly targets chip-level and modular cooling innovations, including two-phase cold plates and thermal resistance reduction.
notes: Supports component-level cooling as an active innovation and bottleneck area.

claim_text: Schneider says data centers are moving past 140 kW per rack with future power densities of 1 MW and more, requiring liquid cooling.
notes: Strong support for liquid cooling as required infrastructure at future densities.

claim_text: Schneider says direct liquid cooling is up to 3,000 times more effective than air and requires an end-to-end approach including sourcing, installation, and maintenance.
notes: Supports the thermal-chain and services bottleneck framing.

claim_text: Schneider's CDU portfolio scales from 105 kW to 2.5 MW and to 10 MW+ through centralized control, showing that CDU scale is becoming a major systems layer.
notes: Supports CDU/facility-side layer as a serious scaling bottleneck.

claim_text: Vertiv is expanding both liquid cooling hardware and fluid-management services, including through PurgeRite, to support high-density AI environments.
notes: Supports service/commissioning as part of the bottleneck.

claim_text: Supermicro says liquid-cooled data centers are expected to rise to 30% of installations and that its rack-level CDU can remove 250 kW per rack.
notes: Useful evidence of rack-level thermal scale, though still vendor-specific.

claim_text: Chip-level thermal interfaces and cold plates are already the single dominant cooling bottleneck.
notes: Evidence supports importance, but not yet that this is the tightest current constraint.

claim_text: Cooling is still mostly an optional efficiency enhancer rather than a required layer for high-density AI deployments.
source_type: inference contradicted by density and liquid-cooling evidence
notes: Current official/vendor evidence points in the opposite direction.
